title: MANGA NGE Will volume 10 be the last
display_title: MANGA NGE Will volume 10 be the last
wikitext:
[]: [NGE] Will volume 10 be the last?             Quote Post by Otaprince » Sat Oct 23, 2004 12:40 pm although I haven't read it yet, Volume 9 features Kaoru, who appeared in episode 24 of the anime. really, all that's left now is for Sadamoto to choose an ending, whether more based on the anime ending or EoE. do you think he'll take one or two volumes to finish the Eva manga?

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Sat Oct 23, 2004 1:20 pm i believe too that the manga will be completed in 10-11 volumes..like you said the ending has left only...

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Sat Oct 23, 2004 1:41 pm Wasn't the official number 12? Unless a revelation is about to occur in the storyline, I don't see how Sadamoto would be able to stretch it out to 12 volumes.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Oct 23, 2004 5:33 pm The official number is 12, as Carl Horn reported from Fanime a year back. There has been no official cancellation of Evangelion. Sadamoto is simply taking an extended hiatus. There is nothing to suggest that the story will be completed in the next volume. Volume 9 surmises the events of episode 22, and not quite half of episode 23. It does not surmise any of episode 24. Kaworu was simply introduced into the plot earlier than he was in the anime. In fact, Kaworu was supposed to enter the plot in episode 20 in the anime, its just that they never got around to it, and pushed it back to episode 24. Sadamoto has essentially gone with the orginal concept in this instance.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Wed Oct 27, 2004 10:23 am I'm really intresed to see how he's going to end things.

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Wed Oct 27, 2004 5:56 pm I just hope they dont strech it out to far. Look what happend to Robocop. :evil:

[]:             Quote Post by Sventevith » Thu Feb 24, 2005 6:42 pm How would it end? Like a mix of EoE & ep. 25-26? Or do you believe he'll take stuff from both of them, and make a whole new ending but with the same subjects/meanings as the anime-endings got +some stuff he thought would fit with the pre-manga he've done? I'm curious...

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Thu Feb 24, 2005 6:44 pm Concedering that the manga is equvlent to Eva lite I say the ending will be easier to understand then 25-26 but a bit less bloody then EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Fri Feb 25, 2005 7:20 pm The ending of the anime was a visual feast. It would be interesting if the final episodes of the manga were nothing but text.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Fri Feb 25, 2005 7:48 pm I agree. But I seriously doubt Sadamoto has the spark to do something that impressive.

[]:             Quote Post by Sventevith » Fri Feb 25, 2005 7:51 pm Nothing but text... hard to believe that ;) Maybe a lot of text, but not plain text... It would be nice to get his viewpoint of the whole thing, where he explains it with the characters in some way....

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Feb 25, 2005 8:35 pm Just wondering judging by the size of each manga who many collected additions will their be by the time they get to the end? Also I think the end of the manga will be a bit easier to understand, but lacking a bit of the depth.

[]:             Quote Post by Sventevith » Fri Feb 25, 2005 8:37 pm DatDude wrote: Just wondering judging by the size of each manga who many collected additions will their be by the time they get to the end? Also I think the end of the manga will be a bit easier to understand, but lacking a bit of the depth.

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Fri Feb 25, 2005 8:47 pm I have faith in Sadamoto. So far, the Evangelion manga has surpassed everything else I've read for a long time (when talking about manga, that is), and I can't imagine anything but an amazing ending of it. Go Sadamoto go! :mrgreen:

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Fri Feb 25, 2005 9:39 pm I can imagine 10 years from now: "Evangelion Renewal - the manga"

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Feb 25, 2005 9:40 pm For the love og god so can I ! >runs in fear<

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Feb 25, 2005 9:52 pm AchtungAffen wrote: I can imagine 10 years from now: "Evangelion Renewal - the manga"

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Thu Mar 03, 2005 7:10 pm Reichu wrote: AchtungAffen wrote: I can imagine 10 years from now: "Evangelion Renewal - the manga"

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Thu Mar 03, 2005 8:19 pm Representing the fundamental events that took place from the point at which the 17th Angel, Tabris, was defeated won't be too tricky. The JSSDF attack, Asuka Launches, Harpy Fight, Shinji Launches, 3rd Impact, Anti-AT Field, Complementation occurs, The beach scene, the end. That's it, when you boil it right down. The only real question - and it's one hell of a biggie - is what Sadamoto-san will put into the 'complementation' scenes. Naturally, what worked on the screen won't work here. There were other scenes in the series which were similar to the type of thing we se during complementation, and Sadamoto managed to do them justice in the manga form, but these scenes are an order of magnitude more complex. I'm not sure, myself, if even 12 volumes is long enough to do justice to the whole 'mindtrip' sequence.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Thu Mar 03, 2005 8:53 pm My worst fear when it comes to the manga is that Sadamoto just go " fuck it" and quits making them. I hope it does not come to that but for gods sake its been like 10 years and he's not done YET. As for him doing the instrumentality scenes, whell he's been a friend of Anno's for a wile mabe he'll call him and get some advice :)
